category_title: Relationship-dependent students sensitive to teacher attitude
minimal_context: John is working on a math assignment and asks the tutor for help with a graphing problem.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is your math task. You need to graph that problem.

STUDENT: I need help with this.

TUTOR: You gotta like, graph that problem with there.

STUDENT: You're just telling me what I need to do, not how to do it.

TUTOR: You just need to complete the work.

STUDENT: [voice sounds angry] You aren't really teaching me anything useful! I don't know, it's just negative all the time, just attitude.

options.A: Teach the student exactly how to do the graphing problem.
options.B: Allow the student to not be rushed, giving him space to stop, think, and start again.
options.C: Ask a direct question about a specific component of the graphing expression that was ignored.
options.D: Allow the student to work at his own pace and take a break when he wants.
